pi_items.source: Web search confirms Kabul is the capital city of Afghanistan (latitude 34.52813, longitude 69.17233). Task description and README explicitly state route starts from Kabul. Verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kabul

Web search confirms Kandahar is a major city in southern Afghanistan in Kandahar Province (latitude 31.6133, longitude 65.7101). Task description and README explicitly state route ends in Kandahar. Verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kandahar

README.md explicitly states: 'Change the **Start Point** from "Current Location" (or "My Position") to "Kabul"' and task description states device's GPS location 'must not' be used as start point. Verifier code (line 129-134) confirms start must not be 'Current Location'.

setup_sygic_gps.sh script (line 105-108) explicitly downloads Afghanistan offline map as first map during environment setup. Web search confirms Sygic GPS supports offline maps for Afghanistan. Verified via https://www.sygic.com/what-is/offline-maps

privileged_info_summary: The task plan_remote_route@1 requires planning a driving route from Kabul (the capital of Afghanistan, located at coordinates 34.52813°N, 69.17233°E) to Kandahar (a major city in southern Afghanistan at 31.6133°N, 65.7101°E). The route must use Sygic GPS Navigation with pre-installed offline maps of Afghanistan. The task explicitly forbids using 'Current Location' or 'My Position' as the start point; instead, the user must manually set the start point to Kabul. The final verification requires the agent to display a route preview screen showing both cities as start and end points with calculated distance and time statistics.
